{{Quote|Leave this place!|Unidentified Noghri guard|<ref name="JKJA">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''</ref>}}
'''Yalara''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Yalara system/Legends|Yalara system]] of the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

==History==
[[File:YalaraDam.jpg|thumb|left|Dam with the [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] on planet Yalara]]
Yalara was originally inhabited by a primitive species of [[Yalaran]]s that were not ready to become part of galactic society. To protect them, Jedi Master [[Broden Kel Verdox]] installed a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] that masked the whole planet from the galaxy.<ref name="JKJA" />

[[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] were sent there, who wiped out the Yalarans defending the device.<ref name="JKJA" />

===Battle of Yalara===
In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], Yalara was under the control of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. While allied with the [[Disciples of Ragnos]], the Empire attempted to lay hands on the cloaking device. However, [[Lore Keeper|Jedi Scholar]] [[Tionne Solusar]] soon rediscovered the planet with her holocron, and, in order to thwart the Imperial plans, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaden Korr]] was sent to [[Mission to Yalara|destroy the mechanism]]. After a short skirmish with Imperial Remnant, Jaden Korr managed ascend to the top of the tower, holding bombs to destroy the device. After a fight with many [[New Reborn]]s and Noghri on the top, Jaden Korr manged to deploy four bombs around the device and successfully destroyed the cloaking device.<ref name="JKJA" />

===Aftermath of the battle===
After returning to the Jedi Academy, Skywalker suspected that Darth Vader, who used many Noghri as slaves and bodyguards, had ordered the Noghri to exterminate the Yalarans and guard the cloaking device. The remaining Noghri on Yalara were returned to Honoghr.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' {{1st}}
